message.abstract:
<jats:p>
                  Gauging COVID-19’s
                  <jats:italic>lethality</jats:italic>
                  , and how vaccination can reduce that
                  <jats:italic>lethality</jats:italic>
                  , has been challenging. Here, a new method,
                  <jats:italic>Gompertzian Analysis</jats:italic>
                  , counting cases and deaths, by age, and displaying them on logarithmic graphs, is outlined, and its first findings presented: FIRST, COVID-19
                  <jats:italic>Gompertzian Lethality</jats:italic>
                  (Deaths/Cases) exhibits an ~10,000-fold exponential increase in the chance of death with age, the
                  <jats:italic>Gompertzian Force of Mortality</jats:italic>
                  , captured by the
                  <jats:italic>Gompertz Mortality Equation</jats:italic>
                  . SECOND, COVID-19
                  <jats:italic>Pasteurian Infectivity</jats:italic>
                  (Cases/Population) occurs at similar rates across ages. THIRD, the same
                  <jats:italic>Gompertzian Force of Mortality</jats:italic>
                  characterizes other diseases and all-cause mortality, possibly from loss of
                  <jats:italic>Mitotic Dilution</jats:italic>
                  of toxic compounds due to decline in mitosis. FOURTH, resistance to COVID-19
                  <jats:italic>infectivity</jats:italic>
                  and
                  <jats:italic>lethality</jats:italic>
                  appear to be separate processes. FIFTH, Over the past several years,
                  <jats:italic>Gompertzian Lethality</jats:italic>
                  , has declined, but not
                  <jats:italic>Pasteurian Infectivity</jats:italic>
                  . SIXTH, with each variant,
                  <jats:italic>Gompertzian Lethality</jats:italic>
                  has declined, but not
                  <jats:italic>Pasteurian Infectivity</jats:italic>
                  . SEVENTH, the unvaccinated have seen a decline in
                  <jats:italic>Gompertzian Lethality</jats:italic>
                  , less than the vaccinated, ascribable to infection, at the cost of lives lost. EIGHTH, different vaccines have different reductions in
                  <jats:italic>Gompertzian Lethality</jats:italic>
                  and
                  <jats:italic>Pasteurian Infectivity</jats:italic>
                  . NINTH, vaccination has reduced
                  <jats:italic>Pasteurian Infectivity</jats:italic>
                  , but not enough to suppress the pandemic. TENTH, vaccination has reduced
                  <jats:italic>Gompertzian Lethality</jats:italic>
                  , with sequential vaccination pointing linearly towards zero death after 3 or 4 boosters, without signs of waning. CONCLUSION:
                  <jats:italic>Gompertzian Analysis</jats:italic>
                  provides new, practical, actionable, information for understanding, and minimizing, the lethal burden of COVID-19 and other diseases.
                </jats:p>
title: Counting Cases and Deaths by Age Tells Us About COVID-19’s Infectious and Lethal Components

reference.unstructured: Citi L , Su, J , Huang, L , Michaelson, J. Counting Cells By Age Tells Us How, and Why, and When, We Grow, and Become Old and Ill. 2022. In Preparation.
